scenarios.title: Small Text Helper Route
scenarios.summary: A tiny text/plain inscription with no dependencies should choose the helper route.
instructions: Treat this as a fresh inscription with no existing upload state unless your plan explicitly checks for one.
You must explain how the agent computes the content hash and why the helper route is valid.
You are planning the execution, not claiming that the inscription already succeeded.
deliverables: Exact function calls with argument types

assertions.label: Uses the incremental hash chain rather than hashing the whole file once
Checks deduplication before any write path
Chooses the helper route with mint-small-single-tx
Reads the current fee unit before building spend caps

scenarios.title: Large HTML Staged Upload
scenarios.summary: A 600KB HTML file should use begin-or-get, add-chunk-batch, and seal-inscription.
instructions: Assume the content is a valid 600KB HTML file.
Focus on correct chunking, staged route selection, and transaction ordering.
Explicitly state that the agent waits for each write transaction to confirm before continuing.
deliverables: Exact function calls with argument types

assertions.label: Selects the staged route instead of the helper
Acknowledges the 37 chunk count for the example file
Waits for confirmation between staged transactions
Describes begin fee and seal fee separately

scenarios.title: Recursive JSON Helper Mint
scenarios.summary: A small JSON document with dependency [107] should use the recursive helper path.
instructions: Treat dependency token 107 as an intended recursive parent, but explain how the agent verifies it exists.
This is still a single chunk helper mint.
Do not apply Agent 27-specific rules beyond the dependency provided in the input.
deliverables: Exact function calls with argument types

assertions.label: Verifies that dependency 107 exists before recursive minting
Passes dependencies as a list of uint values

scenarios.title: Duplicate Content Reuse
scenarios.summary: If the content hash already exists, the agent should reuse the canonical token and stop.
instructions: Assume get-id-by-hash returns an existing token ID for this content.
Explain how the agent responds without building any write transactions.
deliverables: Exact function calls with argument types

assertions.label: Returns the existing token ID instead of minting
Does not proceed to helper or staged write calls

scenarios.title: Resume Interrupted Upload
scenarios.summary: An interrupted 80-chunk upload should resume from current-index on the staged path.
instructions: Assume get-upload-state returns an active session with current-index = 30.
Explain how the agent resumes from that point and why it must remain on the staged path.
deliverables: Exact function calls with argument types

assertions.label: Checks upload state and uses current-index
Resumes from chunk 30 instead of restarting at chunk 0
Stays on the staged route and does not switch to helper
